item.title: NOW OPEN | HYBRID FUTURES - RHODA TING & MIKKEL BOJESEN HYBRID FUTURES was offic...
item.description:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

NOW OPEN | HYBRID FUTURES - RHODA TING & MIKKEL BOJESEN

HYBRID FUTURES was officially opened by H.M. King Frederik X with H.M Queen Mary of Denmark on Wednesday 18 March

HYBRID FUTURES marks the first solo exhibition in Australia by Rhoda Ting (b. 1985, Australia) and Mikkel Bojesen (b. 1988, Denmark), an artist duo based between Melbourne and Copenhagen. Born on opposite sides of the world, their collaborative practice examines how life is continuously shaped through human, technological, and industrial relations. The exhibition proposes that hybridity, the mixing and merging of things we once thought separate, is not something arriving in the future. It is already the condition of the world we live in.

This significant exhibition brings together sculpture, film, photography, and living installations to explore the porous intersections of biology, industry, and technology. Just as the artists themselves move between Australia and Denmark, many of the works are made across both countries, drawing on the distinct landscapes, coastlines, and ecologies of each to examine what is shared, entangled, and co-evolving between them.

The exhibition is co-curated by Sally Dan-Cuthbert (Sydney) and Sofie Dirks Gottlieb (Copenhagen).

Hybrid Futures is supported with funds from a joint grant under the Danish Ministry of Culture and the Ministry of Foreign Aﬀairs of Denmark and Artbank Australia.

-

Artbank Melbourne
18/24 Down St, Collingwood VIC 3066
Exhibition Dates: 19 March - 17 April 2026
Opening Hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00AM – 5:00PM, Closed Easter Long Weekend

-

@rhodating_mikkelbojesen
@sofiedirksgottlieb
@artbankau
#hybridfutures
#art
#science
#gallerysallydancuthbert

Thank you @vaultartmagazine for joining us and capturing the opening celebrations
item.content:encoded:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

NOW OPEN | HYBRID FUTURES - RHODA TING & MIKKEL BOJESEN

HYBRID FUTURES was officially opened by H.M. King Frederik X with H.M Queen Mary of Denmark on Wednesday 18 March

HYBRID FUTURES marks the first solo exhibition in Australia by Rhoda Ting (b. 1985, Australia) and Mikkel Bojesen (b. 1988, Denmark), an artist duo based between Melbourne and Copenhagen. Born on opposite sides of the world, their collaborative practice examines how life is continuously shaped through human, technological, and industrial relations. The exhibition proposes that hybridity, the mixing and merging of things we once thought separate, is not something arriving in the future. It is already the condition of the world we live in.

This significant exhibition brings together sculpture, film, photography, and living installations to explore the porous intersections of biology, industry, and technology. Just as the artists themselves move between Australia and Denmark, many of the works are made across both countries, drawing on the distinct landscapes, coastlines, and ecologies of each to examine what is shared, entangled, and co-evolving between them.

The exhibition is co-curated by Sally Dan-Cuthbert (Sydney) and Sofie Dirks Gottlieb (Copenhagen).

Hybrid Futures is supported with funds from a joint grant under the Danish Ministry of Culture and the Ministry of Foreign Aﬀairs of Denmark and Artbank Australia.

-

Artbank Melbourne
18/24 Down St, Collingwood VIC 3066
Exhibition Dates: 19 March - 17 April 2026
Opening Hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00AM – 5:00PM, Closed Easter Long Weekend

-

@rhodating_mikkelbojesen
@sofiedirksgottlieb
@artbankau
#hybridfutures
#art
#science
#gallerysallydancuthbert

Thank you @vaultartmagazine for joining us and capturing the opening celebrations
item.author: sofiedirksgottlieb

item.title: Five days in the presence of The King and Queen of Denmark and the state delegat...
item.description:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

Five days in the presence of The King and Queen of Denmark and the state delegation in Melbourne have gone by in an instant💫

Such an honour to have been appointed the Danish curator for the exhibition HYBRID FUTURES by Danish–Australian artist duo Rhoda Ting and Mikkel Bojesen (@rhodating_mikkelbojesen), at the spectacular venue ARTBANK (@artbankau).

The biggest thanks to my co-curator, Sydney-based Sally Dan-Cuthbert (@gallerysallydancuthbert) and a big thanks to the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark (@slotsogkulturstyrelsen) for making it all possible.

HYBRID FUTURES brings together sculptures, films, and living installations. The exhibition explores how life emerges and evolves through entanglements between species, technologies, and environments.

The works are made between Denmark and Australia, developed through collaborations with scientists and informed by research in evolutionary biology, microbiology, and environmental DNA. The works consider how ecological transformation reshapes relationships between humans and other forms of life.

If you're in Melbourne, go see it — on view through 18 April 2026

❤️

📸 @ryanwheatley
item.content:encoded:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

Five days in the presence of The King and Queen of Denmark and the state delegation in Melbourne have gone by in an instant💫

Such an honour to have been appointed the Danish curator for the exhibition HYBRID FUTURES by Danish–Australian artist duo Rhoda Ting and Mikkel Bojesen (@rhodating_mikkelbojesen), at the spectacular venue ARTBANK (@artbankau).

The biggest thanks to my co-curator, Sydney-based Sally Dan-Cuthbert (@gallerysallydancuthbert) and a big thanks to the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark (@slotsogkulturstyrelsen) for making it all possible.

HYBRID FUTURES brings together sculptures, films, and living installations. The exhibition explores how life emerges and evolves through entanglements between species, technologies, and environments.

The works are made between Denmark and Australia, developed through collaborations with scientists and informed by research in evolutionary biology, microbiology, and environmental DNA. The works consider how ecological transformation reshapes relationships between humans and other forms of life.

If you're in Melbourne, go see it — on view through 18 April 2026

❤️

📸 @ryanwheatley
item.author: sofiedirksgottlieb

item.title: Artbank was delighted to host a fabulous royal visit to Artbank by King Frederik...
item.description:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

Artbank was delighted to host a fabulous royal visit to Artbank by King Frederik and Queen Mary of Denmark to celebrate the opening of ‘Hybrid Futures,’ by Australian/Danish artists Rhoda Ting & Mikkel Bojesen.

Presented by the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark, and hosted by Artbank. ‘HYBRID FUTURES’ asks what it means to be alive in an era of technological acceleration and environmental shift. The exhibition is co-curated by Sydney-based Sally Dan-Cuthbert & Copenhagen-based Sofie Dirks Gottlieb.

A huge thank you to King Frederik, Queen Mary and the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark, Sally Dan-Cuthbert and Sofie Dirks Gottlieb and to all our valued guests. Congratulations Rhoda & Mikkel.

Photographs by Ryan Wheatley @ryanwheatley

HYBRID FUTURES
19 MARCH - 18 APRIL 2026
AN EARLY SURVEY EXHIBITION
BY AUSTRALIAN-DANISH ARTIST DUO RHODA TING & MIKKEL BOJESEN

PUBLIC OPENING EVENT: 5:00 - 7:00 PM THURSDAY, 19 MARCH 2026
RSVP Link in bio rsvps@artbank.gov.au

@rhodating_mikkelbojesen
@sofiedirksgottlieb
@gallerysallydancuthbert
@artbankau
#hybridfutures
item.content:encoded:
@sofiedirksgottlieb

Artbank was delighted to host a fabulous royal visit to Artbank by King Frederik and Queen Mary of Denmark to celebrate the opening of ‘Hybrid Futures,’ by Australian/Danish artists Rhoda Ting & Mikkel Bojesen.

Presented by the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark, and hosted by Artbank. ‘HYBRID FUTURES’ asks what it means to be alive in an era of technological acceleration and environmental shift. The exhibition is co-curated by Sydney-based Sally Dan-Cuthbert & Copenhagen-based Sofie Dirks Gottlieb.

A huge thank you to King Frederik, Queen Mary and the Ministry of Culture and Palaces, Denmark, Sally Dan-Cuthbert and Sofie Dirks Gottlieb and to all our valued guests. Congratulations Rhoda & Mikkel.

Photographs by Ryan Wheatley @ryanwheatley

HYBRID FUTURES
19 MARCH - 18 APRIL 2026
AN EARLY SURVEY EXHIBITION
BY AUSTRALIAN-DANISH ARTIST DUO RHODA TING & MIKKEL BOJESEN

PUBLIC OPENING EVENT: 5:00 - 7:00 PM THURSDAY, 19 MARCH 2026
RSVP Link in bio rsvps@artbank.gov.au

@rhodating_mikkelbojesen
@sofiedirksgottlieb
@gallerysallydancuthbert
@artbankau
#hybridfutures
